rationale: In the citing paper, Soman and Gourville (2001) is not just listed in references; it is highlighted in the bundling literature review and in Table 2 as one of the few studies with temporal effects. The authors use it to frame a literature gap, arguing that prior bundling work focused mostly on evaluation/consumption while their work examines defection from an associated service. This gives the cited paper a meaningful conceptual positioning role, even though the citing paper does not directly reuse its design or mechanism.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Soman and Gourville (2001) as key prior evidence that bundling can change postpurchase consumption over time. It extends that line by examining product-service bundles and showing effects on service defection over time via a different mechanism (commitment projection).
